adapters.name: Codex Local Collector
adapters.scope: Read Codex Desktop local token_count events and expose 5-hour and weekly capacity windows.
manualChecks: Compare the top bar quota with Codex Desktop account quota UI on Windows and macOS.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Reads local token_count metadata only; does not collect prompts, completions, source files, API keys, or cookies.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Uses bounded local file reads, short local timeouts, and the shared provider refresh interval; no cloud polling or tight loops.

adapters.name: Hermes Local Collector
adapters.scope: Read local Hermes state and optional Xiaomi Token Plan metadata for Hermes quota display.
manualChecks: Verify Xiaomi Token Plan used credits against platform.xiaomimimo.com after refreshing the local cookie.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Reads local Hermes metadata and optional user-provided Xiaomi platform cookie; does not send prompts, completions, source files, workspace contents, API keys, or cookies to the local ingest API.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Uses short local timeouts, caches provider quota reads through the shared provider refresh interval, and avoids per-request UI automation.

adapters.name: Hermes OpenAI-Compatible Bridge
adapters.scope: Proxy local OpenAI-compatible Hermes requests and report response usage.
manualChecks: Point a local OpenAI-compatible client at 127.0.0.1:17668, confirm /health is ok, and confirm usage appears in /snapshot.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Forwards requests to the local Hermes target and reports only usage metadata; prompt and completion text, source files, workspace contents, API keys, and cookies are not posted to /events or stored.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Uses short local timeouts, no watchdog loop, bridge health checked on demand, and best-effort usage posting.

adapters.name: Browser Extension Adapter
adapters.scope: Report browser HUD avoidance rectangles and explicit page-provided usage events.
manualChecks: Load unpacked in Chrome and Edge, test Options connection through /health, and verify non-matching sites do not inject scripts.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Uses precise host permissions and reports only overlay rectangles or explicit usage events; no prompts, completions, source files, workspace contents, cookies, API keys, or page text are uploaded.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Manifest V3 service worker, short local timeouts, throttled MutationObserver, bounded overlay reports, no persistent background page, and no setInterval DOM scanning.

adapters.name: VS Code/Cursor Adapter
adapters.scope: Show compact local health in VS Code-compatible IDE status bars and expose an explicit copy-snapshot command.
manualChecks: Run VS Code Extension Development Host and manually install the VSIX in Cursor.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Reads local /health for status and /snapshot only on explicit copy command; does not read workspace source files, prompts, completions, API keys, or cookies.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Status-bar refresh only, short local timeouts, user-configurable endpoint/token, and no background watchdog.

adapters.name: Node SDK Wrapper
adapters.scope: Give Node and JavaScript tools a best-effort wrapper for reporting usage and overlay data.
manualChecks: Wrap one OpenAI-compatible response, confirm /health is ok, and confirm it appears in /snapshot.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Only sends explicit usage fields supplied by the caller; does not inspect prompts, completions, API keys, cookies, or source files.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Short timeout, no throw by default, bounded request payloads, no polling loop, and no background service.
adapters.disablePath: Do not instantiate the SDK client or set endpoint to disabled in caller config

adapters.name: External Summary Import
adapters.scope: Import already-parsed JSON usage summaries, quota windows, context windows, and credit-plan fields from tools such as TokenTracker, ccusage, or local reports and post compact usage events to the local API.
manualChecks: Run a dry-run import against a trusted TokenTracker or ccusage JSON export, confirm /health is ok, then post it to a running desktop app and confirm /snapshot updates.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Imports token usage, cost, quota, and allowlisted scalar metadata only; it does not copy prompts, completions, API keys, cookies, source files, or arbitrary exported content into /events.
adapters.performanceBoundary: One-shot CLI import with short local timeouts, no watcher, no background process, no polling loop, and best-effort localhost POST.
adapters.disablePath: Do not run npm run import:usage-report or remove the external scheduled job that calls it

adapters.name: MCP Server Adapter
adapters.scope: Let agent clients read current token capacity and optionally post explicit usage events.
manualChecks: Connect an MCP client to scripts/mcp-server.mjs while the desktop app is running.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Reads local /snapshot and posts only explicit usage events; does not collect prompts, completions, source files, API keys, or cookies.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Read-mostly request/response process with short local timeouts, no desktop polling or HUD drawing.
adapters.disablePath: Remove the MCP server from the client config

adapters.name: Agent Skills And Codex Plugin
adapters.scope: Package setup, doctor, and adapter-authoring workflows for agent users.
manualChecks: Install the repo-local plugin in a Codex-compatible plugin environment and run setup/doctor prompts.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Stores workflow instructions only; does not run realtime monitoring, inspect windows, read prompts, completions, source files, workspace contents, API keys, or cookies.
adapters.performanceBoundary: No background runtime, no polling loop, no queues, and no watchdog; only executes when an agent invokes the workflow.
adapters.disablePath: Uninstall or do not enable the agent plugin or skills

adapters.name: Provider-Specific Adapters
adapters.scope: Future adapters for Cursor, Claude, Gemini, LiteLLM, OpenAI billing APIs, credit-plan APIs, and other provider-specific usage or quota surfaces.
manualChecks: New provider adapters must add exact entrypoints, automated checks, and privacy/performance boundaries before moving out of planned status.
adapters.privacyBoundary: Must report only usage and quota metadata; no prompts, completions, source files, API keys, or cookies may be sent to /events.
adapters.performanceBoundary: Must use short timeouts, respect provider cache windows, avoid unbounded polling, and fail quietly when the desktop app is closed.
adapters.disablePath: Provider-specific setting or adapter uninstall path
